summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering financial matters, personnel changes, and community events. Key actions included approving a new interest-bearing savings account with CF Bank to generate investment income, paying a $50,645.18 IRS tax assessment from a 2020 payroll audit, and creating and filling a new part-time grants coordinator position. The board also approved a Sons of the American Revolution ceremony at Meyer Cemetery and accepted two employee resignations.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda – passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (Aug 12 – Sep 12, 2023) – passed
Motion to approve participation in CF Bank interest-bearing (IntraFi/ICS) savings account – passed
Motion to adopt Resolution R-019-2023 accepting tax levy amounts and rates and certifying to county auditor – passed
Motion to accept resignation of Michaela Young (HR Manager) effective 9-9-23 – passed
Motion to accept resignation of Firefighter EMT Colin Weissman effective 8-27-2023 – passed
Motion to increase State of Ohio EMS grant from $770 to $3,232.50 – passed
Motion to allow Sons of the American Revolution ceremony at Meyer Cemetery on October 21, 2023 – passed
Motion to reimburse John Rose $308.51 for truck window damage from service department debris – passed
Motion to increase appropriation of $50,645.18 for IRS tax assessment – passed
Motion to approve payment of $50,645.18 to IRS for tax assessment – passed
Motion to approve Director Meadows APWA membership and travel to APWA Expo Oct 2-3, 2023 ($160) – passed
Motion to enter executive session at 6:49 PM – passed
Motion to approve job description for part-time Project Specialist/Grants Coordinator – passed
Motion to appoint Donna Rosick as part-time Project Specialist/Grants Coordinator effective 9-24-23 – passed
Motion to adjourn at 7:28 PM – passed
action_items: Fiscal Officer Laura Englund and Jen Farmer to visit Hamilton Township to learn procedures for managing the CF Bank savings account
Administrator Rose to vet and present HR manager candidate at next meeting to replace Michaela Young
Director Meadows to coordinate with Sons of the American Revolution (Michael Ogon) on October 21 ceremony logistics at Meyer Cemetery
Fiscal Officer Englund to pay IRS tax assessment of $50,645.18 by September 25 deadline
Fiscal Officer Englund to work with legal counsel and state auditors on potential employee reimbursement for IRS assessment amounts
Director Meadows to attend APWA Expo in Dayton October 2-3
Donna Rosick to begin as part-time Project Specialist/Grants Coordinator effective September 24, 2023
key_amounts: $250,000 conservative initial transfer to CF Bank interest-bearing account
$13,750/year estimated interest on $250,000 at 5.5% Fed funds rate
5.5% – current Fed funds interest rate offered by CF Bank
$50,645.18 – IRS tax assessment for 2020 payroll audit including interest
$3,232.50 – increased State of Ohio EMS grant amount (up from $770)
$308.51 – reimbursement to John Rose for truck window damage
$160 – APWA membership and expo registration fee for Director Meadows
$20/hour – pay rate for new part-time grants coordinator position (~$6,000 annually)
$90,000+ – amount CPAAA has given back to community over 12 years
$48.26 – current monthly interest earned on regular checking account
notable_topics: CF Bank interest-bearing savings account (IntraFi/ICS) to earn investment income on township funds
IRS tax assessment from 2020 payroll audit regarding Medicare withholding and medical opt-out taxation
Sons of the American Revolution ceremony at Meyer Cemetery for six Revolutionary War soldiers (Oct 21)
Creation of part-time Project Specialist/Grants Coordinator position and appointment of Donna Rosick
Resignation of HR Manager Michaela Young and Firefighter EMT Colin Weissman
Increase in State of Ohio EMS grant
Recognition of Sgt. Beckleheimer and Officer Lunsford for assistance in domestic violence arrest
attendees: Dan (likely Trustee Hodges or another official)
Michael Ogon (Sons of the American Revolution, Cincinnati Chapter)
notes: Trustee Allen was absent. Executive session held from 6:49 PM to 7:22 PM to discuss appointment/employment/compensation of public employee. A public commenter made unfounded claims about mRNA vaccine nanotechnology and microwave emissions. The IRS audit finding stemmed from an ADP payroll system issue where blocking Social Security also inadvertently blocked Medicare withholding for employees transitioning from part-time to full-time, and from untaxed medical opt-out payments – issues not caught by prior state audits. New employee James Pruitt and new mower were noted as working well. Officer Chris Davidson congratulated on new baby.
title: BOT 9-12-23
